short_summary: US cuts NATO assets in Europe
rationale: Romania, a Black Sea NATO member bordering Ukraine and Moldova, is directly exposed to any reduction in US military assets supporting NATO European operations.

top_events.title: US to cut air and naval assets deployed for NATO operations in Europe
top_events.summary: Reduction of US military assets in NATO Europe directly affects Romania's security on the Black Sea flank, where Russian naval and air threats remain elevated due to the Ukraine war.

top_events.title: Iran says deal to end fighting with US has 'never been closer'
top_events.summary: A US-Iran agreement could reduce global energy price pressures, offering modest indirect relief to Romanian consumers and the broader EU economy.

top_events.title: Ukraine reclaims territory as it doubles attacks on Russian logistics
top_events.summary: Continued Ukrainian battlefield progress is a positive signal for Black Sea regional security, but qualitatively unchanged from the previous cycle.
